works.title: Ali's Hippeastrums
works.description: The inspiration for this painting comes from the exquisite garden of my cousin Ali on Mt Tamborine.

works.title: Ali's Xanthostamen
works.description: Arranged in my favourite Carrol Boyes coffee mug, this beautiful Australian shrub grows in Ali's native garden on Mt Tamborine.

works.title: Cabbage Patch
works.description: I so enjoy painting produce and marvel at the endless variations of green tones found in this cabbage.

works.title: Lilies from Sue
works.description: Sue arrived for dinner with these lilies in hand, and they were arranged in one of my favourite vases from Spitalfields Market in London.

works.title: Narcissus in Sunlight
works.description: The scent of a flower can transport you back to a special moment in your life. This painting takes me back to my grandfather's bulb garden, when I was just five years old.

works.title: Anna's Nasturtiums
works.description: When I saw this colourful jug at Lynda's home, I immediately knew it had to hold these vibrant nasturtiums. The jug was reluctantly returned!

works.title: Floral Arrangement on Kenzo Scarf
works.description: My absolute favourite scarf, bought at the Kenzo shop in Paris when I travelled abroad as a textile designer. It appears often throughout my artwork and continues to inspire me.
images: images/works/Floral Arrangement on Kenzo Scarf - Oil/01.jpg
images/works/Floral Arrangement on Kenzo Scarf - Oil/02.jpg
images/works/Floral Arrangement on Kenzo Scarf - Oil/03.jpg

works.title: Flowers from the Garden
works.description: A looser oil painting of seasonal flowers from my garden, arranged in a favourite ginger jar discovered at a flea market in Amsterdam.

works.title: Penny's Basque Figs
works.description: I have the most wonderful friends! I first met Penny, an interior designer, when we lived in the village of Ogbourne St George, Berkshire. Last year, I visited her in the Basque region, where she lives in a stunning farmhouse surrounded by a hedge of fig trees.

works.title: Pony Club's Nasturtiums
works.description: Growing wild near the pony club, these cheerful nasturtiums are always a joy to pick as a little posy for my table.

works.title: Protea from Ewan
works.description: These magnificent flowers hold a special place in my heart, reminding me of my birth country, South Africa.

works.title: Cabbage from the Market
works.description: I delight in the unfurling forms of cabbages, which lend themselves so beautifully to watercolour.

works.title: Oxley Market Aubergine
works.description: As a child, purple and green were my favourite colour combination, long before I understood the principles of the colour wheel.

works.title: Oxley Market Produce on Batik
works.description: Oxley Market on a Saturday morning is always bustling, and I couldn't resist these bright yellow persimmons.
images: images/works/Oxley Market Produce on Batik - Watercolour/01.jpg
images/works/Oxley Market Produce on Batik - Watercolour/02.jpg
images/works/Oxley Market Produce on Batik - Watercolour/03.jpg

works.title: Sally's Camellias
works.description: I'm surrounded by wonderful neighbours with old camellia trees, so I'm spoilt for choice when it comes to these beautiful blooms.
